compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Why

`docs/import-export.md` honestly notes that bulk import takes one git commit and
one lock per event, so multi-thousand-ticket imports serialize the write lock and
can take minutes. But there is no consolidated **scale envelope** telling
adopters what the tool can take: how many tickets/events/clones, expected
list/search latency, git object growth, and the maintenance commands that keep a
long-lived store healthy. Popularity changes the load profile (Jira imports,
bulk migrations, many agents, multi-year stores), and adopters need honest
numbers to plan.

## What

Add `docs/scale-envelope.md` documenting, with **representative measured
numbers** produced by a quick local benchmark run during implementation:

- Ticket / event / clone counts the design comfortably supports.
- Import throughput (events/sec) and the per-event commit+lock cost.
- `list` / `search` / replay latency at a few representative store sizes.
- Git object / pack growth expectations on a long-lived `tickets` branch, and
  why `gc.auto=0` is set.
- Compaction thresholds and the recommended maintenance commands
  (`compact` / `compact-all` / `fsck`).

## Scope

One doc (`docs/scale-envelope.md`) + a short reproducible measurement method.
Docs-only: no batched-commit import and no CI benchmark harness (both OUT per the
epic). Cross-link from README and `docs/import-export.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/scale-envelope.md` exists with representative measured numbers for
  counts, import throughput, and list/search latency.
- [ ] It documents git-growth expectations and the compaction/maintenance
  commands.
- [ ] The measurement method is reproducible (documented commands/store sizes).
- [ ] README and `docs/import-export.md` link to it; no benchmark harness is
  wired into CI.
compiled_state.title: Publish a scale-envelope doc with representative measured numbers
